question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user call getNodes() and observe that toNodeAddress() calls addressBookEntry.getNodeAccountId().toAccountID(), and if nodeAccountId is EntityId.EMPTY (due to a missing node table entry), the resulting AccountID may be zero/invalid, causing staking clients to misroute reward claims to the wrong account?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a getNodes() request with a malformed protobuf AddressBookQuery that causes EntityId.of(query.getFileId()) in toFilter() to throw an exception, and observe the specific error message returned via onErrorMap(ProtoUtil::toStatusRuntimeException) to infer internal entity ID ranges used in staking reward accounts?
